definitions.definition: Primary activities are those economic activities which are directly related to the management of land, water, forests, and climate. These activities are also known as extractive or extractive activities.
Agriculture is the cultivation of land for growing crops and rearing livestock. It is one of the primary activities and is essential for the production of food, raw materials, and cash crops.
Forestry is the management of forests for the production of timber and other forest products such as fuelwood, pulpwood, and resin.
Fishing is the activity of catching fish and other aquatic animals from rivers, lakes, and oceans. It is one of the primary activities and is a major source of protein for many communities.
Mining is the extraction of minerals and other geological materials from the earth. It is one of the primary activities and is an important source of raw materials for industry.

Hunting is the practice of catching and killing wild animals for their meat, hides, and other body parts. It is one of the primary activities and is an important source of protein for many communities.

Gathering is the collection of fruits, berries, nuts, and other non-timber forest products from the wild. It is one of the primary activities and is an important source of food and income for many communities.

Livestock rearing is the practice of raising animals such as cows, pigs, sheep, and goats for their meat, milk, and other products. It is one of the primary activities and is an important source of income and food for many communities.
Crop rearing is the practice of growing crops such as wheat, rice, and maize for food and other purposes. It is one of the primary activities and is an essential part of agriculture.
Extractive industries are those economic activities which involve the extraction of natural resources from the earth. Examples include mining, drilling, and logging.
Subsistence activities are those economic activities which are undertaken for survival and are not for profit. Examples include gathering and hunting.
Commercial activities are those economic activities which are undertaken for profit. Examples include agriculture and forestry.
